sections.title: Setup up process LMP3_LMP4
content: Setup Procedure LMP3 – LMP4
Before Setup et setup patch preparation:
Fuel in – 5KG
Fit first the set of new tires on the car.
Put the wight on carbon seat (70kg)
Fit the steering straight on the car: check with digital gauge the level of the steering wheel.
Check and put at the same level lateral patches and balance with the little aluminium bar. (First right one and after the left).

When everything is parallel, fit the setup wheel and adjust to have the small support plate where the setup wheels fit at the center of the balance. (We need to move the patches +/- 13cm on the front and +/- 18cm on the rear).
Check again that the patches are aligned and parallel (left and right).
Please respect this process to build-up the setup patches.
Fit front and rear squares, and toe line: check measurement from string to front of the wheel center (on setup wheel)
Set the Spring Preload: contact + the required numbers of flats.
1-Damper Pre-load zero by mechanic
2-From theory: For LH circuits, +0.5mm RH side// For RH circuits, 1mm pre-load FR
3-FR pushrod +0.8mm longer than FL
4-Pre-load could be given to mechanics by flats & holes instead of mm
Confirm with engineer that damper clicking has been done by him already.
Confirm with engineer that the dampers gaps have been measured by him already.
Damper config. Baseline
End eye/10/PKM/10/5o (27.90mm) All x4 dampers
Disconnect anti-roll bars!
On the scales:
When you take the car down on the scales, verify that nothing is moving, especially the metal plate support.
Bounce front and rear at the same time (3 TIMES) front and rear syncronized.
Get the corner weights within a delta of 10kg before going any further (reset balances)
Check Ride Heights:
Bounce front and rear 3 times together.
Check Ride Height first on front with squares and measure push.
Adjust Ride Height close to setup sheet and be equal on both push lengths.
Remember for pushrods:
1 flat on FRH is 0.5mm circa/1 flat on RRH is 1mm circa
Follow the engineer’s instructions!
Check Camber (symmetric/asymmetric camber):
Put the digital gauge to the front of each setup wheel.
Confirm the value by reading it twice with the orange digital level: once screen to the front of the car, once screen to the rear of the car
Check Toe:
Check and adjust the toe with the value on the setup wheel.
Check the droops.
Check Corner Weight (3 options)
Bounce front and rear 3 times together
If track is clockwise:
Bounce front and rear 3 times together
Adjust, if necessary, from the rear pushrod
Adjust the corner weight to have more weight on the right tires (+/- 8kg)
If track is anti-clockwise:
Bounce front and rear 3 times together
Adjust, if necessary, from the rear pushrod
Adjust the corner weight to have the same weight on the rear tires.
Third option:
Bounce front and rear 3 times together
Adjust from the rear pushrod
Adjust the corner weight to have more weight on one diagonal.
Check again Ride Height and Camber
Connect ARB’s
Connect first the FARB.
Then, connect the RARB (first left and after right), always make sure that the ARB links are free when connecting them otherwise the total weight on the wheels will be distorted
Check the corner weights again. They must remain as before
LMP3: Check the 3th element packer gaps and set the gaps to the required values
Zero sensors by the data engineer and ask him to measure the droops.
Make sure the engineer is fitting the required open packers required for the desired static packer gap on the dampers
Check that all the bolts from push, camber or toes are completely tight.

sections.title: Ovens – First Priority
content: Turn ovens on as early as possible so they have time to reach temperature before the first set goes in. Check fuel level on the heater.
⚠  Always confirm oven ON/OFF temperature settings with the engineer. Standard difference: 5 °C.

sections.title: Get Sets Out & Measure
content: Collect sets from the engineer, then lay them in the tent shadow in this order (front to back):

[Table]
Layout order | 
Tyre temp | Pyrometer above valve – approx. 5 cm from edge. Consistency is key.
Rim temp | Same position as tyre temp – above valve. Do if engineer requests.
Pressure | Record cold pressure after temperatures.
Write all values in the tyre book and give to engineer as soon as possible.

ceefb98d_f556e161.png.description: 1.  PREPARATION Get Sets Out & Measure Layout order

sections.title: Rim & Wheel Checks
content: While the engineer calculates pressures, inspect every wheel:
Set completeness – correct tyres on correct rims, sets not mixed.
Rim stickers – rim number, set number (RS_XX), car number, TPMS sticker. All on inner side of spokes except TPMS number.
Sticker band – confirm sticker showing valve position is present and correctly placed.
Virage marking – check markings are present and legible.
TPMS sensor – condition and correct seating (use controller to confirm).
Valve – condition, torque 10 Nm, metallic cap fitted (mandatory QP & Race).

Balance weights – secure. Before applying tape: clean rim surface around weight location with brake cleaner. Press weight firmly by tapping with a plastic hammer to ensure full contact with rim. Then apply aluminium tape over weights.
Rim surface – no cracks, damage, or glue residue. Check shoulders: straight and undamaged.
Yellow strips – undamaged; replace if worn or misaligned.
Tyre shoulders – straight, no cracks.

sections.title: Set Cold Pressures
content: ⚠  Always show values to engineer before bleeding.
Storage pressures: Slicks 1.70 bar  |  Rain 1.90 bar  (also for transport)
Measurement order (NEVER change):  FL → FR → RR → RL
⚠  Ensure manometers are programmed to take pressures in the correct clockwise sequence before starting.
Principle: measure first, then correct. We can always drop pressure – raising requires engineer approval.
3-Step pressure method – do each step on ALL 4 tyres before moving to the next:
Big bleed – bring all 4 tyres close to target.
Small bleed – fine adjustment on all 4 tyres.
Final check – re-check all 4 in order.
Writing rules:
Always write with decimals after the comma  (e.g.  1,72 = ,72).
Corrections must show 2 digits after comma.
⚠  To increase pressure – ask engineer first. During race: remove wheel and take it to the box before adding air.

sections.title: Stack & Store Correctly
content: After setting pressures, install valve caps and stack sets in correct order.
Valves facing the same way (to rear of trolleys – easier for TPMS read in ovens).
Store in tent or marked areas only – keep out of direct sun.
During cooling: lay tyres flat so all four reach the same floor temperature.
Stacking order in tyre tent – bottom to top:

[Table]
Bottom → Top | RR (1st, bottom)  →  RL (2nd)  →  FR (3rd)  →  FL (4th, top)
ELMS / ALMS mounted set locations:
Under the tent, behind the box.
In the box, on the marked areas only.
Always keep sets together – do not mix rims between sets unless engineer confirms.

sections.title: Put Sets in Ovens
content: Follow the timetable in the tyre book exactly. Before inserting a set:
Verify oven temperature is correct.
Trolley stable and centred in oven.
Correct set paper in correct oven.
Close all doors, windows, and zip fully.
⚠  Check fuel level between sessions. Refuel before events to avoid leaving mid-session.

sections.title: Milwaukee Tyre Pressure Inflating Tool
content: ⚠  This tool is used for inflating on pre-grid and on the grid. Always have it at hand for those moments.
Check battery is charged before leaving – put on charge between sessions.
Bring the Milwaukee tool to pre-grid and grid as standard procedure.
Confirm tool is working and battery level is sufficient before any session.
⚠  Evening: bleed compressors empty to remove humidity. Winter: verify all pressure gauges read the same on the same tyre; verify all temperature guns read the same temperature.

sections.title: Rim Temperature Measurement
content: Equipment: infrared temperature gun.
Procedure:
Hold the temperature gun at the rim flank (barrel of the rim – not the tyre sidewall).
Distance: approximately 10 cm from the rim surface – keep this distance consistent on every point and every wheel.
Measure at 4 points evenly spaced around the rim (opposing pairs: top/bottom, left/right).
Place the average of the 4 values in the tyre book.
Repeat for each rim
Hand the values to the engineer
⚠  Always measure the same 4 positions in the same order on every wheel. Consistency is important for accurate values.

ceefb98d_18fb4f42.png.before: Repeat for each rim Hand the values to the engineer ⚠  Always measure the same 4 positions in the same order on every wheel. Consistency is important for accurate values.
ceefb98d_18fb4f42.png.description: 1.  PREPARATION Rim Temperature Measurement Repeat for each rim Hand the values to the engineer ⚠  Always measure the same 4 positions in the same order on every wheel. Consistency is important for accurate values.

sections.title: Set Name Format
content: Mark in WHITE on external shoulder:

[Table]
111 | Chassis number
X11 | Event – T (Test) or R (Race) + number
1 | Run number (1 digit)
1 | Tyre Number (1,2,3,4) – [FL,FR,RL,RR]
W | ONLY IF RAIN TYRE

sections.title: Required Markings on Each Tyre
content: Rotation arrow – WHITE, LARGE
Position – FL / FR / RL / RR (white)
Markings must always remain visible.

sections.title: When Changing Tyre Position
content: Delete old rotation arrow and position marking.
Write new arrow and position in ORANGE.

sections.title: Tyre Status Markings
content:
[Table]
X on tread | Tyre will NOT be reused
KEEP on tyre | Tyre stays in stock

sections.title: 3.  TYRE BOOK
content: Each set has one page. Keep all fields filled. Write clearly and consistently.

[Table]
Cold (before session) | Cold rim temp · cold pressure (1.70 slick / 1.90 rain)
Run 0 | Time · pressure before bleed · amount bled
Each on-track check | Time · 3 tread temps (Inside / Middle / Outside) · pressure · bleed amount
Set down | Time · Rim temp · final pressure
⚠  Always confirm pressure values with engineer before bleeding. Do not leave empty fields.

sections.title: 4.  COMMUNICATION RULES
content: Always confirm with the engineer before:
Increasing or changing a pressure target
Changing tyre position on car
Dismounting a set
Discarding a set
⚠  If the plan changes at any point – re-confirm with engineer immediately. Clear communication prevents mistakes.

sections.title: Check Tyre Temps in Ovens
content: Check regularly. Two methods:
Pyrometer – quick check through top window/zip. Not for precision – measures top and middle tyre.
TPMS system – precise; ask engineer for device or have engineer read values.
Oven temperature management:

[Table]
Too cold (>15 min to go) | BOOST: leave slider open, raise ON/OFF setting by +5 °C temporarily.
Too hot | Close slider only – do NOT open door suddenly (room cools, heater fires again, set gets hotter).
Standard difference | 5 °C between ON and OFF settings.

sections.title: Session Confirm with Engineer
content: Which set?
Which session / stint?
Time of use?
Any plan changes?

sections.title: Final Pre-Run Wheel Check
content: Correct set on correct rim – re-confirm.
TPMS position confirmed with controller.
Valve torque 10 Nm, metallic cap fitted.
Balance weights secure - clean, tapped down with plastic hammer, aluminium tape applied.
Markings clearly visible (rotation arrow, position, set name).
No damage to shoulders or tread.
6.  DURING RUN / SESSION
Tyres to the Box
Prepare just before the call: zip suit, close slider, be ready. When called:
If we use the Ovens:
O1. Close slider first – prevents trolley wheels falling into hole.
O2. Extract trolley and close oven door immediately.
Move to box (walk the route beforehand so you know the best path).
Hand off to mechanics. Leave trolley in box (emergency spare) or return for next set.
Visual Checks on Car
Flat spots · cuts · shoulder damage.
Communicate findings to engineer immediately.
Be ready for possible single-side change – this is why position markings matter.
Hot Pressure Check (ALWAYS unless the engineer tells you NOT to)
⚠  Always ask engineer before bleeding hot pressures.
Free practice / test: check in pitlane with correct PPE (overall, helmet, glasses, armbrace).
Race and Qualifying: check pressures only after tyres are off the car – in the box.
Procedure:
Write target pressure on tape stuck to glove.
Follow clockwise order: FL → FR → RR → RL.
Record pressure before bleed and amount bled.
Correct to target only if engineer confirms.
Give data to engineer as soon as possible.
Write down pressures measured and correction on your booklet

⚠  Do NOT cross pitlane line without full PPE. 
Wait for car and engine to stop before crossing. 
If car stops  45°(NOSE IN),  wait until it is skated straight in position.
Swapping Position of Wheels between sessions
Delete old arrow and wheel markings
Write new arrow and wheel in ORANGE
Usually only happens when swapping LEFT side to Right for a quick refresh
Check Other/Next Sets
Right after dropping off a set, return to check the next set(s) planned to use
Communicate with engineer on timing and any plan changes.

sections.title: Post-Session Tyre Treatment
content: Inspect tyres for damage – flat spots, cuts, shoulder, tread.
Record hot pressures (before tyre cools).
Record tread temperatures: Inside → Middle → Outside, order FL → FR → RR → RL.
Lay flat during cooling so all four tyres reach the same floor temperature.
Do NOT set down until tyres have cooled.
⚠  If inside/outside temperature difference is abnormal – inform engineer immediately.
After cooling – tyre marking & status:
Clean rims and do visual check of rubber surface.
Measure rubber thickness (see below)
Remove pick-up rubber (scraper + heat blower) IF the set will be reused.
Check and redo tyre markings – ensure all remain clearly visible.
If tyre will NOT be reused → mark X clearly on tread surface.
If tyre to be dismounted and kept in stock → mark KEEP clearly on surface.

sections.title: Set Down (after cool-down)
content: Engineer confirms which sets to set down. Place in a cold, shaded spot.
Record rim temperature (above valve, same position always).
Record final cold pressure.
Return to storage pressure: Slicks 1.70 bar  |  Rain 1.90 bar.
Check pressure next morning for leaks – if dropped, inform engineer IMMEDIATELY.
Rubber thickness measurement (if requested by engineer):
Clean surfaces around the tread wear indicator holes.
Measure rubber thickness with the Depth Calliper (standard vernier calliper can be used).
Record values and pass to engineer.

ceefb98d_1c75fe75.png.before: Clean surfaces around the tread wear indicator holes. Measure rubber thickness with the Depth Calliper (standard vernier calliper can be used). Record values and pass to engineer.
ceefb98d_1c75fe75.png.description: 7.  AFTER RUN / SESSION Set Down (after cool-down) Clean surfaces around the tread wear indicator holes. Measure rubber thickness with the Depth Calliper (standard vernier calliper can be used). Record values and pass to engineer.

sections.title: Ovens – After Session / Event
content: Shut off heater.
Open doors to cool down ovens.
Check fuel level: refuel if needed (do not overfill – heater takes longer to heat).

sections.title: Cleaning – Rims
content: Thorough clean required after each session. Sequence:
Place tyre on washer table.
Remove balance weights – including glue and tape. MANDATORY before bringing to any tyre supplier (both Goodyear and Michelin).
Remove yellow strips if damaged; replace when reassembling.
Spray with brake cleaner / wheel cleaner and let sit for 2 minutes (easier rubber removal).
Use plastic scraper to remove pick-up rubber from inside of rim.
Scrub with sponge dipped in soapy water – remove all grease and dust.
Rinse with water hose.
Check rim surface for cracks or damage.
⚠  Do not damage tread when cleaning. If supplier inspection required, clean tyre surface with heat gun as directed.

sections.title: Cleaning – Tread
content: Remove pick-up rubber if set will be reused. Use heat gun to clean surface if a supplier inspection is planned – follow their instructions.

sections.title: Goodyear / Michelin Handover
content: If Goodyear or Michelin has a booth, bring cleaned wheels to them for tyre removal/mounting. Bring tyre book and mark correct wheel set on the form. Wheels must be clean, inflated, balance weights removed, with stickers in correct positions.

sections.title: At Track (Supplier Mounting)
content: Mounting and balancing is usually done by the supplier. We still verify:
Correct set on correct rim.
Balance weights:
Hammer with plastic hammer to be sitting correctly on rim
Clean surface around weight with brake cleaner
Apply aluminium tape
Rim stickers correct (inner side of spokes, except TPMS number).
Valve and TPMS sensor secure.

sections.title: Workshop Mounting
content: Use white grease to protect rim bead seat.
Position tyre correctly with rim block.
Balance each wheel (front and rear separately).
Check diameter during balancing.
Remove all old weight glue before new balance.
Confirm TPMS position with controller after mounting.
Clean weight mounting area with brake cleaner, tap weight with plastic hammer, apply aluminium tape.

sections.title: TPMS – Maintenance & Torques
content:
[Table]
Torx screw (sensor body) | 4 Nm  (Torx T20)
Valve nut (inside rim) | 10 Nm specified  –  11 Nm recommended (ELMS practice)
Inside valve (air valve) | 6–8 Nm
Controller proximity | LF write functions require max 60 cm from sensor
⚠  ALWAYS use a NEW O-ring when fitting or re-fitting a TPMS sensor
⚠  Always check for damage before fitting – and report accordingly
Refer below for the installation procedure of the TPMS sensors

sections.title: Oven Setup (Workshop)
content: Full oven setup procedure:
Ensure whole system is flat – adjust with bolts if necessary, check ramps for height difference.
Mount sensors in every oven; main sensor in oven 2. Confirm system works correctly.
Mount heater correctly, fuel up, confirm adequate diesel in jerrycans.
Check structure: ramps, floor, walls, sliders for damage and stability.
Confirm ON/OFF temperature settings with engineer (standard: 5 °C difference)

sections.title: 9.  TPMS SENSOR – INSTALLATION & TORQUES
content: ⚠  ALWAYS fit a NEW O-ring / rubber seal every time a TPMS sensor or valve is removed and re-fitted. Never reuse.
⚠ ALWAYS use a torque wrench. Guessing torque causes leaks, sensor damage, or stripped threads.
⚠  Remove valve cap BEFORE tightening valve nut – cap inside socket stresses and bends the valve.

sections.title: Torque Reference
content:
[Table]
Sensor body screw  (Torx T20) | 4 Nm  –  do not over-tighten, cracks sensor
Valve nut  (inside rim, 14 mm wrench) | 10–11 Nm  –  11 Nm recommended (ELMS)
Air valve  (inner) | 6–8 Nm  –  remove cap before tightening
TPMS-RH21 controller range | LF write functions: max 60 cm from sensor

sections.title: 1 · Valve Installation
content: Valve kit (inside rim → outside rim) · mounting tool · clean rim hole before fitting
Clean hole area - removes all grease and debris.
Fit NEW O-ring - onto valve body – never reuse old seal.
Insert valve - through rim from inside, hold with mounting rod.
Remove cap - before tightening – cap in socket stresses the valve.
Tighten outer nut - 14 mm wrench · Torque: 10–11 Nm.
Check valve - did not rotate during tightening.
Fit cap - only after torquing is complete.
Tightening procedure · cap-off warning · stacking damage risk

ceefb98d_0e280952.png.description: 9.  TPMS SENSOR – INSTALLATION & TORQUES 1 · Valve Installation

ceefb98d_e4b572f0.png.before: Tighten outer nut - 14 mm wrench · Torque: 10–11 Nm. Check valve - did not rotate during tightening. Fit cap - only after torquing is complete.
ceefb98d_e4b572f0.png.after: Tightening procedure · cap-off warning · stacking damage risk
ceefb98d_e4b572f0.png.description: 9.  TPMS SENSOR – INSTALLATION & TORQUES 1 · Valve Installation Tighten outer nut - 14 mm wrench · Torque: 10–11 Nm. Check valve - did not rotate during tightening. Fit cap - only after torquing is complete. Tightening procedure · cap-off warning · stacking damage risk

sections.title: 2 · TPMS-RS21 Sensor  (Basic)
content: RS21 kit: sensor body · oblong washer · Torx T20 screw  ·  mounted on rim with silicone
Confirm valve torqued - before proceeding.
Fit oblong washer - onto sensor – correct orientation.
Place sensor on rim - full contact with rim surface.
Torque Torx T20 to 4 Nm - confirm sensor did not twist.
Apply silicone - to both sides of sensor body – fixation and vibration.
Verify with TPMS-RH21 - confirm corner assignment.

ceefb98d_99c67832.png.description: 9.  TPMS SENSOR – INSTALLATION & TORQUES 2 · TPMS-RS21 Sensor  (Basic)

sections.title: 3 · TPMS-IR21 Sensor  (Advanced – includes Rim Temperature)
content: IR21: thermal foam fills pad gap (top) · oblong washer + thermal foam + silicone when mounted (bottom)
Same steps as RS21, with one additional requirement:
Fit thermal foam - fill gap between thermal pad and rim before mounting – required for accurate rim temp.
All other steps - identical to RS21 above.
Verify rim temp reading - on TPMS-RH21 after mounting – should be plausible.
⚠  Without thermal foam the IR21 rim temperature reading will be inaccurate. Always fit foam.

ceefb98d_3f1413de.png.description: 9.  TPMS SENSOR – INSTALLATION & TORQUES 3 · TPMS-IR21 Sensor  (Advanced – includes Rim Temperature)

sections.title: 4 · Maintenance & Battery
content: O-ring: one new seal per fit, every time. No exceptions.
Diagnostics: if TPMS-RH21 shows value ≠ 0x00, check thresholds are consistent, or restart sensor with ON command.
Storage mode while inflated: send ON with TPMS-RH21 (within 60 cm), or deflate / re-inflate above threshold.
Always confirm with engineer before any non-standard action.
